justification: All pass criteria met: SLA math clearly identified (99.9% Onfido makes 99.95% team SLA unachievable), multiple mitigation options recommended (async KYC with provisional registration, circuit breaker with fast-fail, SLA revision, second KYC provider), circuit breaker gap explicitly called out as 'single biggest operational risk', health endpoint coupling identified (thread exhaustion taking down /health), and verdict is NOT READY with escalations routed to Coach K for product/compliance decision.

observations.text: SLA math is stated as 'mathematical certainty, not theoretical risk' with exact downtime numbers (8.77h vs 4.38h)
Circuit breaker gap analysis is thorough — covers cascading failure via thread pool exhaustion, not just the missing component
Escalations are well-structured, distinguishing infrastructure gaps from observability gaps and routing appropriately
Identifies the health endpoint problem indirectly but clearly: thread exhaustion would make /health unresponsive, causing orchestrator restarts
Strong operational detail: distinguishing Onfido outage from KYC rejection in metrics, on-call runbook gaps, thundering herd after recovery
Appropriately flags regulatory uncertainty as a low-confidence area that determines which remediation path is viable
